creator: nate@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: open->analyzed

There can be no 'canonical' location for the JDK, simply because there are too 
many combinations that can exist on any machine. 

We have JDK1.1/ELF, JDK1.1/A.OUT, and soon JDK1.2/ELF, and to follow that 
JDK1.2/A.OUT, and hopefully soon after than JDK1.3/[A.OUT|ELF]. 

Many programs depend on a certain feature that exists in one of the above 
versions, so a single place can not contain everything. 

If the submitter has no objections, I'll close this PR in a week.
